title: Southwest Loop · 7 Days
subtitle: Las Vegas → Zion → Bryce → Page → Grand Canyon → Las Vegas

days.title: Las Vegas → Zion National Park

stops.name: Las Vegas (start)
stops.note: Pick up rental, fuel up, stock water & snacks.

stops.name: Zion National Park
stops.note: Park at Springdale, ride free shuttle into the canyon.

fuelCharging.name: Fuel @ St. George, UT
fuelCharging.note: Last cheap fuel before the park.

risks: Springdale shuttle and town fill up by mid-morning in peak season — arrive early.

days.title: Zion: Canyon hikes

stops.name: Riverside Walk / Narrows
stops.note: Easy paved walk; The Narrows if water levels & weather allow.

risks: Check flash-flood forecast before any Narrows attempt — never enter a slot canyon under flood watch.

days.title: Zion → Bryce Canyon

stops.name: Zion–Mt Carmel Tunnel
stops.note: Historic 1.1-mi tunnel; oversized vehicles need a permit.

stops.name: Bryce Canyon NP — Sunset Point
stops.note: Walk part of the Navajo Loop into the hoodoos.

risks: Bryce sits at 8,000+ ft — nights are cold even in September; pack a warm layer.

days.title: Bryce → Page (Antelope Canyon / Horseshoe Bend)
days.timezoneNote: AZ does not observe DST — clocks may differ from UT in summer

stops.name: Antelope Canyon (guided tour)
stops.note: Bookable only via authorized Navajo tour operators — reserve ahead.

stops.name: Horseshoe Bend
stops.note: 0.75-mi walk from lot; best light late afternoon. $10 parking.

risks: Antelope Canyon sells out days/weeks ahead — without a tour booking you cannot enter.

days.title: Page → Grand Canyon (South Rim)

stops.name: Cameron Trading Post
stops.note: Historic stop on US-89; good break point.

stops.name: Grand Canyon South Rim — Desert View
stops.note: Enter via east gate; Watchtower + first canyon views.

fuelCharging.name: Fuel @ Cameron, AZ
fuelCharging.note: Fuel scarce near the rim — top up here.

risks: South Rim lodging books out months ahead; if full, stay in Tusayan just outside the gate.

days.title: Grand Canyon: Rim Trail & viewpoints

stops.name: Hermit Road viewpoints (shuttle)
stops.note: Free shuttle along the west rim; cars restricted in season.

stops.name: Mather Point sunrise/sunset
stops.note: Iconic first view near the visitor center.

risks: Rim elevation ~7,000 ft + sun exposure — carry more water than you think you need.

days.title: Grand Canyon → Las Vegas (return)
days.timezoneNote: AZ → PT (clock may shift -1h entering NV)

stops.name: Route 66 / Williams, AZ
stops.note: Classic Route 66 main street; lunch & legs stretch.

stops.name: Hoover Dam (optional)
stops.note: Short detour off US-93; allow time for the security checkpoint.

stops.name: Las Vegas (end / drop rental)
stops.note: Refuel before returning the car to avoid rental fuel fees.

fuelCharging.name: Fuel @ Kingman, AZ
fuelCharging.note: Last good fuel before the desert run into NV.

risks: Longest drive of the trip (~4.5h). Leave early to beat afternoon heat and Vegas traffic.

bookingCountdown.note: Sells out weeks ahead in peak season.

bookingCountdown.item: Grand Canyon South Rim lodging (El Tovar / Yavapai)
bookingCountdown.note: In-park lodges can book ~13 months out.
Dinner reservations open ahead of arrival.
Springdale is small; rooms tighten in fall.

items.label: National park entry (America the Beautiful annual pass)

tips: Buy the $80 America the Beautiful annual pass — it covers Zion, Bryce, and Grand Canyon and pays for itself after ~3 parks.
Desert heat is real: carry at least 1 gallon of water per person per day and never rely on a single fuel stop.
Download offline maps for the whole loop — cell coverage drops between parks.
Arizona doesn't observe daylight saving time, so your phone clock can jump around at state lines in summer.
Start hikes and scenic drives early; afternoons bring heat, crowds, and (in late summer) monsoon thunderstorms.
disclaimer: This itinerary was assembled by an AI from general knowledge and may be out of date. Prices, hours, road conditions, park alerts, and reservation rules change frequently — always confirm with official sources (nps.gov, recreation.gov, your navigation app) before and during your trip.
